title: Explore Tokio's async Rust runtime capabilities.

items.correct: Evaluates different compilation failures based on feature flags.
distractors: Resets a data structure and its related components to their default states.

Creates a Guard that holds an optional Context and modifies the shared tasks of a Scheduler upon being dropped. Takes existing Tasks from SharedState and initializes them in Current when called with given arguments.
Initializes a new threaded processing system with created resources and spawning mechanism.

items.correct: Resets a data structure and its related components to their default states.
distractors: Checks if types can be sent, unpinned, or statically referenced.
Measures and prints the execution time of a function `f()` in different threading contexts (single-threaded, multi-threaded with 1, multiple threads).
Creates an inner unparker from a raw pointer and then unparks it before forgetting about its memory management responsibility.

items.correct: Initializes a new instance with the provided capacity and read status.
distractors: Checks if a join is interested and not already having a waker set, sets the first available waker on an object state after attempting to update it.
Checks a condition and stores it as cached when if it's false.
Retrieves a cached item, refreshes it if necessary based on initialization comparison, or returns an error.

items.correct: The code handles an internal conversion of a potentially missing value into its containing type and records it as an action to read an error if present.
distractors: Adds a key-value pair to an entry list and returns whether it was removed after removal from another collection based on the provided unique identifier.
Checks if there are enough messages to send; sends a message and returns success if not.
Resets a data structure and its related components to their default states.

items.correct: The code snippet sets up a non-blocking sleep timer and selects between two futures: another future that ignores an 'if' clause or panics.
distractors: Calculates and converts a duration since an event started to milliseconds, panicking if it's in the distant past.
Pauses and then resumes time measurement twice consecutively.
Measures and prints the execution time of a function `f()` in different threading contexts (single-threaded, multi-threaded with 1, multiple threads).

items.correct: The code checks if the host obtained from a local address lookup is IPv4 or IPv6 and asserts it matches an expected SocketAddr.
distractors: The code removes a node from the middle of a doubly-linked list and cleans up its pointers.
Copies the first four bytes of a byte slice into a buffer and checks that it is done correctly.
Initializes a structure with default starting index and pointers for linked nodes, ready slots count set to zero.

items.correct: Increments a poll count by 1 using atomic fetch-add operation and then polls for an event with context handling.
distractors: Checks if a future is ready and pins it using an external crate function.
Sets position and capacity of an object to zero.

Checks if there was an error in the last write attempt and returns it; updates state based on operation type, handles pinning for busy reading or writes result back to sender. Adjusts internal buffer accordingly. Returns appropriate readiness status after handling read/write/seek operations.
